title: Neeraj A vs State Of Kerala on 11 January, 2016
doc:
Neeraj A vs State Of Kerala on 11 January, 2016

Bench: P.R.Ramachandra Menon, Dama Seshadri Naidu

        

 
IN THE HIGH COURT OF KERALA AT ERNAKULAM

                                PRESENT:

            THE HONOURABLE MR.JUSTICE P.R.RAMACHANDRA MENON
                                   &
            THE HONOURABLE MR. JUSTICE DAMA SESHADRI NAIDU

        TUESDAY, THE 27TH DAY OF SEPTEMBER 2016/5TH ASWINA, 1938

                      WP(C).No. 31270 of 2016 (G)
                      ----------------------------


PETITIONER:
-----------

            NEERAJ A,
            SIVA KRIPA, MAITHANAM, VARKALA.


            BY ADVS.SRI.B.S.SWATHY KUMAR
                    SMT.ANITHA RAVINDRAN
                    SRI.HARISANKAR N UNNI
                    SMT.S.SIKKY
                    SMT.T.RESHMA

RESPONDENTS:
--------------

          1. STATE OF KERALA
            REPRESENTED BY SECRETARY, HIGHER EDUCATION DEPARTMENT,
            SECRETARIAT, THIRUVANANTHAPURAM, PIN - 695 001.

          2. THE COMMISSIONER OF ENTRANCE EXAMINATIONS
            5TH FLOOR, HOUSING BOARD BUILDING, SHANTI NAGAR,
            THIRUVANANTHAPURAM-695 001.


            R BY GOVERNMENT PLEADER SRI.RAJASEKHARAN NAIR

       THIS WRIT PETITION (CIVIL)  HAVING COME UP FOR ADMISSION  ON
27-09-2016, THE COURT ON THE SAME DAY DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING:
 
WP(C).No. 31270 of 2016 (G)
----------------------------

                       APPENDIX

PETITIONER(S)' EXHIBITS
-----------------------

EXHIBIT P1     COPY OF THE APPLICATION SUBMITTED BY THE PETITIONER FOR
ADMISSION TO PROFESSIONAL DEGREE COURSE, 2016, DATED 11.1.2016.

EXHIBIT P2     COPY OF THE RELEVANT PORTION OF THE PROSPECTUS FOR
ADMISSION TO PROFESSION DEGREE COURSE.

EXHIBIT P3     COPY OF THE OPTION LIST FOR ADMISSION TO PROFESSIONAL
DEGREE COURSE-2016.

EXHIBIT P4     COPY OF THE ALLOTMENT MEMO TO ADMISSION TO PROFESSIONAL
DEGREE COURSE, DATED 18.9.2016.

EXHIBIT P5     COPY OF THE VACANT SEATS AS REPORTED BY THE COLLEGES
(PROVISIONAL).

EXHIBIT P6     COPY OF THE PRESS RELEASE OF THE 2ND RESPONDENT DATED
19.9.2016 & ENGLISH TRANSLATION.

EXHIBIT P7     COPY OF THE REPRESENTATION FILED BY THE PETITIONER
BEFORE THE 2ND RESPONDENT DATED 23.9.2016 & ENGLISH TRANSLATION.

RESPONDENTS' EXHIBITS    nil
-----------------------



                             true copy


                                               P.S.TO JUDGE
 


                P.R.RAMACHANDRA MENON &
                   DAMA SESHADRI NAIDU, J.
                    ------------------------------------------
                      WP(C) No.31270 of 2016
                     ---------------------------------------
             Dated this the 27th day of September, 2016

                               JUDGMENT

Ramachandra Menon, J.

The petitioner is before this Court challenging clause-iii of Ext.P6 Press Release issued by the second respondent with regard to the spot admission for the first year MBBS 2016-2017; in so far as the same virtually prevents the petitioner from participating in the process, he having already obtained admission in a self-financing institution by name Mount Zion Medical College, as borne by Ext.P4.

2. The petitioner was an aspirant to obtain admission to the first year MBBS course and on participating in the common entrance examination, he got placement at the appropriate level, based on the merit. In furtherance to the admission process pursued by the second respondent, the petitioner came to be allotted to the Mount Zion Medical College at Adoor, WPC No.31270/2016 2 Pathanamthitta, as per Ext.P4 allotment Memo, which is in respect of the 50% merit candidates allocable to the Government quota. The petitioner remitted the fees, with liberty to have further allotment based on higher option. While so, the petitioner came across Ext.P6 spot allotment as per the Press release issued by the second respondent intending to hold the spot selection on 22nd of September 2016. According to the petitioner, his candidate was not considered, by virtue of the adverse stipulation, particularly, under clause-iii of Ext.P6, to the effect that the case of the persons like the petitioner who had already got admission will not be consdiered for the purpose of spot admission. This, according to the petitioner, is contrary to the terms of Ext.P2 Prospectus and hence, the challenge against the said clause in Ext.P6.

3. Heard the learned counsel for the petitioner and the learned Senior Government Pleader appearing for the respondents.

4. The learned Senior Government Pleader appearing for the respondents submits that the idea and understanding of the petitioner is wrong and misconceived, particularly, as to the scope of Ext.P2 prospectus. It is stated that the stipulation WPC No.31270/2016 3 under 'clause iii' of Ext.P6 is in conformity with the stipulation as contained in clause 11.6.9 of Ext.P2 prospectus, which reads as follows:

Spot Allotment : Spot allotment/admission for filling up the remaining vacant seats, if any, may be conducted after the completion of th eonline allotment process. A qualified candidate included in the rank lists concerned is entitled to attend the spot allotment/admission process. Candidates who have obtained admission in Government Merit seats for a course shall not be eligible to participate in the Spot Allotment for that particular course. But a candidate admitted in Government Merit Seat in a Government controlled Self Financing College is allowed to participate in the Spot Admission for a Government College for any course. The courses under Engineering stream are listed in Annexure 11(1)(b). The Commissioner for Entrance Examinations is authorised to issue necessary orders regarding Spot Admission/Allotment in due course.
It is also brought to the notice of this Court by the learned Senior Government Pleader that the grievance of the petitioner, as projected in Ext.P7 representation dated 23.9.2016, is to have allotment to some other private self financing institutions and by virtue of the very nature of the admission procedure in respect of spot allotment and the requirements involved, it is not possible to have the prayer acceded to.

5. After hearing both sides, this Court finds that there is no dispute with regard to the right of option and to have allotment based on the higher options, depending upon the merit of the WPC No.31270/2016 4 candidates concerned. At the same time, with an intention to see that no seat is let to be lapsed in the process of admission, affecting the rights and interest of the institution and to accommodate the maximum number of students, necessary safeguards have been made. If the person already admitted in a self-financing institution is given the benefit of spot admission notwithstanding the riders contained in the notification, the seat in the former Institution will have to be vacated. Various clauses have been provided in the prospectus, agreements and such other orders to ensure the rights and liberties of the parties in this regard and the entire exercise will be toppled down, if the version of the petitioner is accepted and given effect to. That apart, by virtue of the consideration of the matter based on the need of the hour and the limited time frame available, it is not possible for entertaining such prayer and to have it finalised after issuing notice to all concerned.

6. The only question to be considered is whether 'clause-iii' of Ext.P6 is contrary to the stipulations in Ext.P2 prospectus as contended by the petitioner. The petitioner has not referred to clause-11.6.9 of Ext.P2 prospectus anywhere in the writ petition. On comparing the relevant clauses, i.e. Clauses 11.6.9 in Ext.P2 WPC No.31270/2016 5 prospectus and clause - iii of Ext.P6, we find that clause-iii of Ext.P6 is in confirmity with clause under 11.6.9 of Ext.P2. In such circumstances, we find that there is no much force in the submissions made from the part of the petitioner.

7. Another aspect we have noted is that, the petitioner has not chosen to implead the Institution in which he has already got admission in the party array. Similarly, the spot admission is stated as over and the persons who got the spot admission are also not in the party array. As such, the relief sought for by the petitioner is not liable to be entertained. We do not find any merit in the writ petition and the same is dismissed accordingly.




                         Sd/- P.R.RAMACHANDRA MENON, Judge

                            Sd/- DAMA SESHADRI NAIDU, Judge
